title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What Is Critical Illness Cover: All You Need To Know
Being healthy is crucial to achieving any life goal. While one part of being healthy is exercising and eating healthy, the other element is to financially prepare for any illness or eventuality.
The increasing incidences of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India further make it essential that one plan for critical illnesses. According to the health journal Lancet, NCDs accounted for 61% of the deaths in 2016 compared to 38% in 1990. NCDs include critical illnesses like heart diseases, cancer, diabetes, and chronic kidney diseases, among others.
Detection of a critical illness in you or a family member is not only emotionally debilitating but also financially draining for the entire family and can derail your life goals. With the cost of healthcare expenses increasing almost at the double the pace of inflation, the financial burden that critical illness in the family brings can be as challenging to deal with as the emotional trauma. However, you can always plan ahead for such financial stress.
What Is A Critical Illness?
A critical Illness can be one of the many serious ailments, which require lengthy medical procedures, including surgeries, and other pre- and post-hospitalization care to be fully cured. Some of them can be chronic in nature and may require lifelong medication and treatment. These medical procedures and treatments, many of which are only conducted in large private hospitals, cost a fortune.
Add to this the cost of medication, regular follow-ups with doctors and alternative treatments and one critical illness in a family can cause irreparable damage to your finances, if you have not taken additional protection.
One estimate suggests that 85% of the working population does not have instant cash of Rs 5 lakh for treatment in case of detection of a critical illness. No wonder then that 7% of the families are pushed to below poverty line every year due to unexpected healthcare expenses.
Features Of Critical Illness Benefit
The salient features of the critical illness cover are as follows –
The cover is available as an optional rider. You can choose the same for coverage against major life threatening illnesses.
The claim is paid only if the life insured suffers from an illness or undergoes a treatment that is covered under the plan.
The sum assured is paid on diagnosis of the critical illness
The coverage is usually available for s specific tenure under the life insurance policy if you opt for the critical illness rider.
The premium that you pay for the cover is allowed as a deduction from your taxable income. You can claim the deduction under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act, 1961, up to Rs.25,000 if you are below 60 and Rs.50,000 if you are 60 years and older.
You can use the critical illness benefit received to meet any financial need that you might have.
Critical illness cover is usually available for adults aged 18 years and above
There is usually a survival period over which the life insured should be alive after being diagnosed with the critical illness to become eligible for the claim payout.
How To Choose The Perfect Critical Illness Cover?
To choose the perfect critical illness cover, here are some things that you should keep in mind –
Choose a plan that covers the a higher number of illnesses and medical treatments. The larger the number of illnesses covered, the better the protection you would receive.
Choose a sum assured which is optimal enough to meet the expensive treatment costs and/or any other financial liability that you might have.
Compare the premium vis-à-vis the coverage offered by the different critical illness insurance plans. Choose a plan that offers a competitive premium rate without compromising on coverage.
Look for plans that can be renewed till you are older. In other words, choose plans which have a higher maturity age so that you can be covered longer.
Opt for plans that have a shorter survival period so that you can get the claims earlier.
Why Should You Buy Health Insurance With Critical Illness Cover?
Typically, people buy mediclaim policies to fend off any financial emergency due to healthcare reasons. Purchasing a mediclaim policy is a good start, but it has its limitations. A mediclaim policy covers only the hospitalization related expenses, which may not be enough especially when a serious disease like cancer or heart disease is detected.
You, therefore, need a different strategy. Health insurance plans with critical illness cover offers a lump sum amount on detection of a critical illness to take care of both hospitalization and non-hospitalisation related expenses. A critical illness cover ensures that you don't need to dip in your savings for medical reasons. They cover a wide range of diseases, which may vary from insurer to insurer.
Bajaj Allianz has recently launched Bajaj Allianz Life Health Care Goal, which provides a holistic health insurance with critical illness cover for the entire family. One can opt for a different sum assured for each family member and in case you need to claim sum assured for one family member, it does not bring down the sum assured of the other members. Healthcare Goal covers 36 critical illnesses and one can also opt for Return of Premium in case you would like to get back the premiums paid in case of no claim at the end of the policy.
A critical illness cover takes care of your hospitalization and other medical expense and is the best way to protect your finances from any medical expense, which usually catches you unaware.
Conclusion
A critical illness benefit can add an extra layer of protection and cover medical emergencies. The lump sum benefit that you get can be used to pay for your financial obligations or treatments, depending on your needs. So, understand what the benefit is all about and choose it with your life insurance policy as a rider or opt for a standalone plan for adequate coverage.
FAQs
Can I purchase a critical illness benefit once I am diagnosed with an illness?
No, you cannot purchase the benefit after being diagnosed with an illness. The critical illness benefit covers uncertain illnesses, not ones that have been certainly diagnosed.
What are the documents required for a critical illness rider?
You might have to submit a medical examination report and a health questionnaire to buy the critical illness rider.
Does term insurance cover critical illness?
Traditional term insurance plans only offer a death benefit upon demise of the life assured. However, you can opt for add-on riders like the critical illness rider with your term plan for coverage against critical illnesses listed under the plan.
Is there any medical test required before purchasing a critical illness rider?
The requirement of medical tests depends on your age, existing medical conditions, the sum assured chosen for the rider and other factors. The insurance company would underwrite your policy and then determine whether medical tests are needed or not. The tests are not mandatory as they depend on individual policies.
Reference
https://incometaxindia.gov.in/tutorials/20.%20tax%20benefits%20due%20to%20health%20insurance.pdf
Other References

https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/tdmc/your-money/all-about-critical-illness-plans-and-how-to-choose-one/articleshow/51810014.cms
https://www.etmoney.com/learn/insurance/how-much-critical-illness-insurance-do-i-need/
https://www.bajajallianz.com/health-insurance-plans/critical-Illness-insurance.html
Disclaimer-

full_pdf_text:
What Is Critical Illness Cover: All You Need To Know
Being healthy is crucial to achieving any life goal. While one part of being healthy is exercising and eating healthy, the other element is to financially prepare for any illness or eventuality. The increasing incidences of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India further make it essential to have one plan for critical illnesses. According to the World Health Organization, Non communicable diseases (NCDs) kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths globally1. NCDs include critical illnesses like heart diseases, cancer, diabetes, and chronic kidney diseases, among others. Detection of a critical illness in you or a family member is not only emotionally debilitating but also financially draining for the entire family and can derail your life goals. With the cost of healthcare expenses increasing almost at the double the pace of inflation, the financial burden that critical illness in the family brings can be as challenging to deal with as the emotional trauma. However, you can always plan ahead for such financial stress. What Is A Critical Illness? A critical Illness can be one of the many serious ailments, which require lengthy medical procedures, including surgeries, and other pre- and post-hospitalization care to be fully cured. Some of them can be chronic in nature and may require lifelong medication and treatment. These medical procedures and treatments, many of which are only conducted in large private hospitals, cost a fortune. Add to this the cost of medication, regular follow-ups with doctors and alternative treatments and one critical illness in a family can cause irreparable damage to your finances, if you have not taken additional protection.
What is Critical Illness Benefit?
The critical illness benefit is an optional rider available under some life insurance plans, that pays a pre- defined sum assured upon the diagnosis of a critical illness, listed under the plan. This rider enhances the protection under the base plan to offer a comprehensive coverage to the life assured.
Features of Critical Illness Cover
The salient features of the critical illness cover are as follows –
1. The cover is available as an optional rider which can be bought with a Life Insurance Plan, for
added coverage. You can choose the same for coverage against major life threatening illnesses as covered under the rider. 2. The claim is paid only if the life insured suffers from a critical illness specified under the rider
and undergoes a treatment for the same. 3. The sum assured is paid on diagnosis of the critical illness 4. The coverage is usually available for a specific tenure as defined under the critical illness rider. 5. The premium that you pay for the cover is allowed as a deduction from your taxable income.
You can claim the deduction under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act, 1961 under old regime of Tax, up to Rs. 25,000 if you are below 60 and Rs. 50,000 if you are 60 years and older. 6. You can use the critical illness benefit received to meet any financial need that you might have. 7. Under some plans, there may be a waiting period, cooling off period or survival period to
become eligible for the benefit payout, subject to policy terms and conditions.2
How to Choose the Suitable Critical Illness Cover?
To choose the suitable critical illness cover, here are some things that you should keep in mind –
1. Choose a critical illness plan or rider that covers a higher number of illnesses and medical
treatments. The larger the number of illnesses covered, the better the protection you would receive. 2. Choose a sum assured which is optimal enough to meet the expensive treatment costs and/or
any other financial liability that you might have. 3. Compare the premium vis-à-vis the coverage offered by the different critical illness insurance
plans. Choose a plan that offers a competitive premium rate without compromising on coverage. 4. Look for plans that can be renewed till you are older. In other words, choose plans which have a
higher maturity age so that you can be covered longer. 5. Opt for plans that have a shorter survival period so that you can get the claims earlier.
Conclusion
A critical illness benefit can add an extra layer of protection and cover medical emergencies. The lump sum benefit that you get can be used to pay for your financial obligations or treatments, depending on your needs. So, understand what the benefit is all about and choose it with your life insurance policy as a rider or opt for a standalone critical illness plan for adequate coverage.
FAQs
1. Can I purchase a critical illness benefit once I am diagnosed with an illness?
No, you cannot purchase the benefit after being diagnosed with an illness. The critical illness benefit covers uncertain illnesses, not ones that have been certainly diagnosed.
2. What are the documents required for a critical illness rider?
You might have to submit a medical examination report and a health questionnaire to buy the critical illness rider. 3. Does term insurance cover critical illness?
Pure term insurance plans only offer a death benefit upon demise of the life assured. However, you can opt for add-on riders like the critical illness rider with your term plan for coverage against critical illnesses listed under the rider. 4. Is there any medical test required before purchasing a critical illness rider?
The requirement of medical tests depends on your age, existing medical conditions, the sum assured chosen for the rider and other factors. The insurance company would underwrite your policy and then determine whether medical tests are needed or not. The tests are not mandatory as they depend on individual policies.
Reference
1. https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/noncommunicable-diseases
2. https://business.outlookindia.com/personal-finance/insurance/reasons-why-its-good-to-
have-a-critical-illness-cover-in-addition-to-your-health-insurance-policy
Other References
1. https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/here-is-what-you-should-know-about-
critical-illness-cover-11660152937906.html 2. https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/tdmc/your-money/all-about-critical-illness-plans-and-
how-to-choose-one/articleshow/51810014.cms 3. https://www.etmoney.com/learn/insurance/how-much-critical-illness-insurance-do-i-need/ 4. https://www.bajajallianz.com/health-insurance-plans/critical-Illness-insurance.html
Disclaimer-
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
